title: crataegus fulleriana var. miranda
intro: Crataegus fulleriana var. miranda, also known as the hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular-protective effects, derived from its rich content of flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant are numerous, including lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular function, and reducing the risk of heart disease, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss, improve digestion, and boost immune function. Hawthorn is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or supplement, with the leaves, flowers, and berries being used in herbal preparations, often in combination with other heart-healthy herbs.
